# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI pipeline with multiple stages where build artifacts are passed between jobs. The build job creates artifacts (binaries, configs) and deployment jobs download and execute them. Store artifacts for 30 days for team access.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
- stage_name1 # define stages here using names like 'build', etc... (or any you want to use). You can have multiple in a file, and they will run sequentially because of the order defined below if necessary.  If not required then remove this line too! This is optional but recommended for clarity
- stage_name2 # define stages here using names like 'build', etc... (or any you want to use). You can have multiple in a file, and they will run sequentially because of the order defined below if necessary.  If not required then remove this line too! This is optional but recommended for clarity
- stage_name3 # define stages here using names like 'build', etc... (or any you want to use). You can have multiple in a file, and they will run sequentially because of the order defined below if necessary.  If not required then remove this line too! This is optional but recommended for clarity
- stage_name4 # define stages here using names like 'build', etc... (or any you want to use). You can have multiple in a file, and they will run sequentially because of the order defined below if necessary.  If not required then remove this line too! This is optional but recommended for clarity
- stage_name5 # define stages here using names like 'build', etc... (or any you want to use). You can have multiple in a file, and they will run sequentially because of the order defined below if necessary.  If not required then remove this line too! This is optional but recommended for clarity
- ...continue as per your requirement adding stages one by one with names like 'build', etc... (or any you want to use) upto stage_n, where n could be from 10..59 inclusive. Use the same pattern above only replace `stage_*` and add a number or name starting point based on current count plus in this example used as "*".
